name: reddit-developer-sentiment-mcp
title: Reddit Developer Sentiment—What Devs Really Think (MCP Server)
description: Deterministic developer-Reddit sentiment for any brand, tool, or library—sentiment, mention volume, themes, and feature requests across public developer subreddits, via 4 agent-callable MCP tools.

input.title: Reddit Developer Sentiment (MCP) input
input.description: Optional Standby configuration. All fields are optional so the Actor boots with no input — but Reddit API credentials are effectively required to return data rather than credential errors.

redditClientId.title: Reddit Client ID (required in practice)
redditClientId.description: Client ID of a Reddit 'script'/installed app (application-only OAuth). Create one at https://www.reddit.com/prefs/apps — the ID is the short string shown under the app name. Reddit's API rejects unauthenticated requests, so without credentials the tools return a credential error rather than data. Recommended for Standby: also set REDDIT_CLIENT_ID/REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables so they persist across restarts.

redditClientSecret.title: Reddit Client Secret (required in practice)
redditClientSecret.description: Secret of the Reddit app paired with the Client ID above. Found on the same https://www.reddit.com/prefs/apps page. Stored encrypted; never logged or returned by the Actor.

defaultSubreddits.title: Default subreddits
defaultSubreddits.description: Developer subreddits searched when a tool call does not specify its own. Names are normalized (an 'r/' prefix is stripped and they are lowercased). 1–25 entries.

windowDays.title: Default window (days)
windowDays.description: Trailing look-back window (1–90 days) over which mentions are read, when a tool call does not specify its own. Maps to Reddit's day/week/month/year time buckets, then filtered to the exact day count.

cacheTtlMinutes.title: Cache TTL (minutes)
cacheTtlMinutes.description: How long a query's fetched Reddit payload is reused before refetching. Lower = fresher reads; higher = fewer Reddit API calls. All query tools share the same cached payload per query.

maxItems.title: Max items per request
maxItems.description: Upper bound on items returned by a single tool call (Reddit caps a listing at 100). Per-call 'limit' arguments are clamped to this value.
